text.title: Supply Chain Risk Checklist Spike Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Supply Chain Risk Checklist Spike methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Every report MUST score all 7 signals (license, maintainer count, last release, CVE history, stars-to-downloads, transitive depth, vendor lock-in); skipping any signal forces `score: NULL` and marks the report `incomplete`.

rationale.source: Sonatype State of the SC 2023; OpenSSF Scorecard

rule.rationale: Single-signal evaluation misses every major supply-chain incident of 2018-2024; 7 signals cover the failure surface.

rule.statement: CVE history, last release, maintainer count MUST be retrieved within 24h of report emission; older data triggers `data_freshness: stale` and downgrades confidence.
rule.rationale: Stale CVE data is the most common cause of false-clean evaluations; 24h is the maximum tolerable staleness for security-relevant data.

rule.statement: License signal scores 0 for SPDX IDs in operator allowlist, 2 for GPL-family or non-listed permissive, 3 for unrecognised / proprietary / SSPL / BUSL / custom; custom licenses always score 3.

rationale.source: SPDX list; FSF / OSI; SSPL controversy

rule.rationale: Custom licenses cost more in legal review than they save; SSPL/BUSL block commercial SaaS use - using them is a launch-blocker.

rule.statement: If direct dep adds >50 transitive packages (npm) or >25 (PyPI), TRANSITIVE-DEPTH MUST score >=2.
rule.rationale: A single direct dep dragging in 200 transitives is a structural risk no operator can audit.

rule.statement: STARS-TO-DOWNLOAD-RATIO MUST score >=2 when GitHub stars > 10x monthly registry downloads.
rule.rationale: High stars + low downloads = curiosity, not adoption; the maintenance model is weak and vulnerable to social engineering.

rule.statement: Every adoption MUST attach the report with verdict (green/yellow/red) + owner + decay date (re-evaluate by); naked adoption is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without a decision record, vendor accumulation is unauditable; decay dates force periodic re-evaluation.

rule.statement: Yellow or red verdicts MUST come with explicit remediation: `pin-and-monitor`, `fork`, `replace`, or `decline`; a verdict without remediation is rejected.
rule.rationale: Verdicts without remediation produce paperwork; explicit remediation forces an actual decision.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if all entries in `## Applies If` hold and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec - decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
